data.body: DONE (config-loop, commit f05f9665). Config resolution is on the command hot path (verify gate + ticket.display_mode + every CLI invocation route through load_config). Added two process caches, both staleness-safe: (1) _TOML_CACHE — parsed-TOML memo keyed on (path,mtime_ns,size); the discovery [tool.rebar]-presence probe and the table read now SHARE one parse (kills the pyproject double-parse flagged in the 43a0 review); edits miss the cache. (2) _RESULT_CACHE — resolved Config keyed on (root, cwd-when-root-implicit, env-signature, cli-signature); each entry stores stat-tokens of the files it READ, and a warm hit re-stats ONLY those known paths (no walk, no re-parse) and re-resolves if any changed/vanished — so an in-process config EDIT (incl. tightening the verify gate) is always honored in long-running hosts. ConfigError is NEVER cached (fail-closed gate re-evaluates every call). reset_config_cache() forces a fresh walk; conftest clears caches per test. MICROBENCHMARK (pyproject [tool.rebar], 300 iters): cold p50=64.7us/p95=73.2us -> warm p50=7.0us/p95=7.2us (~9.2x). ACs: [x] microbenchmark cold+warm p50/p95 recorded; [x] parsed/discovered ONCE + cached, no per-read re-parse/re-walk (tests: warm-skips-walk, no-double-parse, toml-cache-dedup); [x] upward walk bounded at .git (test_deep_tree_walk_is_bounded, 40-deep subtree); [x] no hot-path regression (warm ~9x faster; warm-budget test: 1000 resolves <0.5s); [x] perf regression guard tests added. Subagent review (2 rounds): initial flagged CRITICAL (result cache not invalidated on edit -> stale verify gate in MCP server) + MAJOR (root=None/cwd bleed); BOTH remediated via stat-token freshness validation + cwd-in-key and re-verified RESOLVED, no residual blockers. Full offline suite green: 1899 passed, 9 skipped.
